transcript.text: Thank you.
Hey, Vivek, how's it going?
Hey, Jackson, how are you?
Good, good.
Another chaotic, busy week.
A couple of product launches, an e-book launch, you know.
Well, nothing ever changes, does it?
But it's good.
No, no, it does not.
What about you?
Good, good.
Things are busy.
They're doing some work outside the place on stage.
So the internet might randomly cut off.
I apologize.
I hope it doesn't happen.
But yeah.
Fingers crossed.
Yeah.
It happened for like five, like a couple minutes on my previous call.
But yeah.
Don't think that should be the case.
Mara might join later because she has a conflict.
So I think, let me share my screen.
We can get started.
Great.
I think Connor, Connor says coming on the invite, but...
We'll see.
I know he's heads down right now.
So probably join a bit later as well.
No worries.
So yeah.
We staged and published five articles this week along with a model review page.
13 are awaiting your review.
And we are on track to deliver seven this week.
Just an internal programming note.
So Ismail is who you've been...
You often chat with him regarding staging, et cetera.
with often chat with chat You chat You chat You often
Internal channel, he is moving to another account within the agency.
So basically, we'll have Nika come back.
don't know if you've worked with Nika before, but he used to be on Galileo.
I did not.
But yeah, either way, there's not going to be much of a disruption here.
just that Nika is going to be sending those messages in sort of his mail from next week onward.
I just wanted to let you know about that.
Moving on to the performance side of things.
So we're pretty much in a holding pattern, as you can see.
Looks like, you know, the tag issues and attribution has been fixed in GA4.
We're kind of holding slightly below the all-time highs that we did.
One reason for this is obviously the volume of net new pages has decreased quite a bit compared to when, you know, we had our exponential sort of increase.
And mostly it's refreshers now, as well as the older pages seem to be hitting, like, pretty much as much performance as we're going to get from them.
So we're done.
Going deeper into refreshes just to see what could really move the needle, as well as trying to prioritize new topics that would likely have a huge search impact.
But generally speaking, we are in a good position.
It's just that we're just trying to figure out how to get even better, generally speaking.
Branded and non-branded impressions have been decreasing over the past couple of weeks.
This is kind of normal, mainly because of Google's strive to reduce bot traffic.
So they've been making a lot of updates, and the declining impressions has been one of the causes of that.
Again, it's not been like a precipitous drop by any means.
It's just been a slightly less gradual sort of a drop.
We think that should stabilize, and generally it's not affecting our rankings or our traffic in any negative way.
So it just seems to be eliminating bot traffic.
In terms of cluster performance, guardrails is...
The governance cluster is a bit volatile right now.
Last week was a bit down in terms of the number of sessions.
The main pillars which are driving our traffic, which is evaluations, reliability, are holding positions quite well.
With regards to keywords from SEMrush, we have 146 evaluation-related keywords in the top 10, and we are ranking for 328 keywords in the top 10 compared to Arise's 904.
So, yeah, it's sort of, we're making slow progress over there, but generally it's a bit up and down.
In terms of LLM visibility, the LLM referral traffic is also sort of holding in the same pattern, in the same range as overall website traffic.
In terms of visibility for the prompts that we're tracking, it's the same position there as well.
We're pretty much neck and neck with brain trust and making up ground to Arise.
Forrest,
The individual categories for agent evals, reliability, troubleshooting, and safety and risk management, we are pretty close to the top three.
Observability is where we're lagging, of course, and we're trying to close that gap with new content as well as the refreshes that we've been slating over the past few weeks.
So generally not much of movement over this past week.
Things are pretty much the same as last week there.
Any questions I've been answered about any of this?
No, I mean, this all makes sense.

It looks like continuing to prioritize observability as a category makes the most sense from a content perspective, and I know some of the refreshes I outlined are aligned with that, so that should help at least a little bit.
Other updates, we got the articles down to eight currently waiting for review, and we'll try to clear out the queue, and we're just down.
I'm a couple of reviewers, so it's taking a little bit longer.
Besides that, all of this makes a lot of sense, and I'm not super worried about it.
It also looks like we're seeing a really solid increase in branded clicks right now, which I would have to guess is from the other Galileo rebranding.
So that is a big positive that I think internally people will be excited about.
Yeah.
Yeah, was nice to see we finally got the top spot, so that's pretty neat.
Yeah, I mean, looking right now, looks like we have more than double our usual URL clicks for branded impressions for the homepage.
That's phenomenal.
Well, it's good that we have the groundwork in place, so we could sort of take advantage of that, so that's pretty good news.
Just wanted to give you an update on the Reddit workflows and Medium republishing.
I think Connor had this question last week.
week.

So the TLDR here is like the service that we currently offer is essentially Reddit monitoring, so mentions of your product, and we do have reporting connected to that, that surfaces different subreddits, and we can sort of treat that essentially as a content gap monitoring kind of a resource.
So that is one service and slash workstream that we offer.
We can republish to Medium as well.
That is a separate workstream, so there will be an additional cost for that, but we're already doing it for some of our other clients.

The, you know, if you want to combine these two into a full package, I guess the full package will kind of look like GrowthX runs the subreddit for Galileo, monitors upcoming topics, et cetera, monitors content gaps, and of course takes care of, you know, repurposing that content from your blog or publishing it to Medium, et cetera.
That would be the full.
Service package, essentially.
So that's kind of what we're doing right now for our clients in terms of Reddit and Medium.
So just wanted to give you an update on that.
Sweet.
So you guys, the full package is like you monitor Reddit, you run a subreddit, republish to Medium.
Did I miss anything there?
Yeah, that's the full package.
Yeah.
So a light version of that would essentially be just monitoring the subreddit and then surfacing any content.
gaps that might occur.
And the Medium republishing would be a separate service slash work stream.
Got it.
Okay.
Do you know what that package typically costs all together?
I can get back to you on the pricing with that.
Like I'll send a little update in Slack with the post-meeting notes.
Cool.
Yeah, that works for me.
And I'm sure Connor and I can talk about it.
Cool.
Well, looks like Jason responded.
Cool.
Asking, how should we track our visibility in LLMs relative to competitors?
That is what Scrunch is for.
I'll respond to that.
I'll drop a link to Scrunch as well so that he can take a look at it.
So, yeah.
Yeah, that would be great.
And then just forward thinking of what we should say as far as what our plan is to catch up to some competitors.
But it looks like we're already pacing upward a bit, so I'm not super worried about it personally.
And since they have more broad reach of, like, different high traffic terms, I'm sure it's something we can regain over time.
Yeah.

Well, hopefully the Model Hub, once we, you know, once it's fully scaled, which should happen by the end of December, I think that should bring in significant amounts of traffic because those are, like, pretty high terms in SEO.
So, the rest of them, I feel like a lot of our topics are super esoteric and they don't really...
Kind of show up in terms of SEO keywords, so it's a bit more challenging, but yeah, we're, I mean, we've got results so far, so pretty sure that we can keep expanding that, so.
Totally.
Yeah, that and competitive pages, more pillar pages, et cetera, et cetera, I'm not super worried about it.
think the strategy we have in place right now makes sense, and I'll also review what's in the article generation pipeline and see if we have to move anything around, but last I looked, it's solid.
Yep.
Cool.
So yeah, that's it.
I will follow up with the pricing after checking internally, and yeah, I'll send that over with the post-call updates.
Awesome.
Well, thank you so much, Vivek.
Much appreciated.
Cool.
Thanks, Jackson.
Bye.
No worries.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Review content performance, pipeline, and new service options.](https://fathom.video/share/jjJxzRz4qEnp2Nc7XtA99VbF3iGQ-Ag9?tab=summary&timestamp=273.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Performance is stable, but growth has plateaued.** The focus is now on optimizing existing content (refreshes) and creating new high-impact topics to break this holding pattern.](https://fathom.video/share/jjJxzRz4qEnp2Nc7XtA99VbF3iGQ-Ag9?tab=summary&timestamp=385.0)
  - [**The recent Galileo rebranding is driving a major traffic spike.** Branded clicks have more than doubled, validating the brand's new visibility and the SEO groundwork in place to capture it.](https://fathom.video/share/jjJxzRz4qEnp2Nc7XtA99VbF3iGQ-Ag9?tab=summary&timestamp=610.0)
  - [**"Observability" is the primary content gap.** It lags competitors in LLM visibility, making it the top priority for new content and refreshes.](https://fathom.video/share/jjJxzRz4qEnp2Nc7XtA99VbF3iGQ-Ag9?tab=summary&timestamp=540.0)
  - [**GrowthX offers new Reddit/Medium services.** Pricing for a full package (monitoring, community management, republishing) will be provided for Galileo's review.](https://fathom.video/share/jjJxzRz4qEnp2Nc7XtA99VbF3iGQ-Ag9?tab=summary&timestamp=653.0)

## Topics

### Content Pipeline & Team Update

  - [**Production:** 5 articles and 1 model review page published this week.](https://fathom.video/share/jjJxzRz4qEnp2Nc7XtA99VbF3iGQ-Ag9?tab=summary&timestamp=338.0)
  - [**Backlog:** 13 articles are staged for Galileo's review; 7 more are due this week.](https://fathom.video/share/jjJxzRz4qEnp2Nc7XtA99VbF3iGQ-Ag9?tab=summary&timestamp=338.0)
  - [**Team Change:** Ismail is moving to another account. Nika, a former Galileo team member, will take over staging communications starting next week.](https://fathom.video/share/jjJxzRz4qEnp2Nc7XtA99VbF3iGQ-Ag9?tab=summary&timestamp=351.0)

### Performance Review & Strategy

  - [**Overall Traffic:** Stable, but growth has plateaued.](https://fathom.video/share/jjJxzRz4qEnp2Nc7XtA99VbF3iGQ-Ag9?tab=summary&timestamp=385.0)
      - [**Cause:** The initial surge from new pages has ended; the focus is now on optimizing existing content (refreshes) and creating new high-impact topics.](https://fathom.video/share/jjJxzRz4qEnp2Nc7XtA99VbF3iGQ-Ag9?tab=summary&timestamp=385.0)
      - [**Note:** A recent drop in impressions is attributed to Google's bot traffic reduction efforts and does not negatively affect rankings or traffic.](https://fathom.video/share/jjJxzRz4qEnp2Nc7XtA99VbF3iGQ-Ag9?tab=summary&timestamp=440.0)
  - [**Branded Clicks:** A significant increase (more than double usual) is attributed to the Galileo rebranding, validating the brand's new visibility.](https://fathom.video/share/jjJxzRz4qEnp2Nc7XtA99VbF3iGQ-Ag9?tab=summary&timestamp=610.0)
  - [**LLM Visibility:**](https://fathom.video/share/jjJxzRz4qEnp2Nc7XtA99VbF3iGQ-Ag9?tab=summary&timestamp=516.0)
      - [**Position:** Neck-and-neck with Brain Trust, making slow progress on Arise.](https://fathom.video/share/jjJxzRz4qEnp2Nc7XtA99VbF3iGQ-Ag9?tab=summary&timestamp=516.0)
      - [**Gap:** "Observability" is the main area lagging competitors.](https://fathom.video/share/jjJxzRz4qEnp2Nc7XtA99VbF3iGQ-Ag9?tab=summary&timestamp=540.0)
      - [**Driver:** The Model Hub, scaling by EOY, is expected to capture high-value SEO terms and significantly boost traffic.](https://fathom.video/share/jjJxzRz4qEnp2Nc7XtA99VbF3iGQ-Ag9?tab=summary&timestamp=821.0)
  - [**SEO Metrics (SEMrush):**](https://fathom.video/share/jjJxzRz4qEnp2Nc7XtA99VbF3iGQ-Ag9?tab=summary&timestamp=495.0)
      - [**Top 10 Keywords:** 328 total vs. Arise's 904.](https://fathom.video/share/jjJxzRz4qEnp2Nc7XtA99VbF3iGQ-Ag9?tab=summary&timestamp=495.0)
      - [**Evaluation Keywords:** 146 in the top 10.](https://fathom.video/share/jjJxzRz4qEnp2Nc7XtA99VbF3iGQ-Ag9?tab=summary&timestamp=495.0)
      - [**Cluster Performance:** "Governance" is volatile; "Evaluations" and "Reliability" are stable traffic drivers.](https://fathom.video/share/jjJxzRz4qEnp2Nc7XtA99VbF3iGQ-Ag9?tab=summary&timestamp=475.0)

### New Service: Reddit & Medium

  - [**Context:** Answering Connor's previous question on Reddit/Medium services.](https://fathom.video/share/jjJxzRz4qEnp2Nc7XtA99VbF3iGQ-Ag9?tab=summary&timestamp=653.0)
  - [**Service Tiers:**](https://fathom.video/share/jjJxzRz4qEnp2Nc7XtA99VbF3iGQ-Ag9?tab=summary&timestamp=653.0)
      - [**Light:** Reddit monitoring → surfaces content gaps.](https://fathom.video/share/jjJxzRz4qEnp2Nc7XtA99VbF3iGQ-Ag9?tab=summary&timestamp=744.0)
      - [**Add-on:** Medium republishing (separate cost).](https://fathom.video/share/jjJxzRz4qEnp2Nc7XtA99VbF3iGQ-Ag9?tab=summary&timestamp=686.0)
      - [**Full Package:** Reddit monitoring, community management, and Medium republishing.](https://fathom.video/share/jjJxzRz4qEnp2Nc7XtA99VbF3iGQ-Ag9?tab=summary&timestamp=697.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Vivek:** Provide pricing for the full Reddit/Medium service package.](https://fathom.video/share/jjJxzRz4qEnp2Nc7XtA99VbF3iGQ-Ag9?tab=summary&timestamp=759.0)
  - [**Vivek:** Respond to Jason's Slack question on LLM visibility tracking, linking to Scrunch.](https://fathom.video/share/jjJxzRz4qEnp2Nc7XtA99VbF3iGQ-Ag9?tab=summary&timestamp=778.0)
  - [**Jackson:** Review the article pipeline to prioritize content that addresses the "Observability" gap.](https://fathom.video/share/jjJxzRz4qEnp2Nc7XtA99VbF3iGQ-Ag9?tab=summary&timestamp=573.0)
  - [**Jackson:** Discuss the Reddit/Medium service options with Connor.](https://fathom.video/share/jjJxzRz4qEnp2Nc7XtA99VbF3iGQ-Ag9?tab=summary&timestamp=759.0)

action_items.description: Post Slack update w/ Reddit+Medium pricing + Scrunch link; then reply to Jason re: LLM visibility
